# Key Magic 0.1.0

Key Magic 0.1.0 is the first public stable release of the project. Because there is no earlier public stable tag, this note serves as a curated product baseline rather than a traditional change log from a previous release.

## What Key Magic Delivers

- Process-aware shortcut blocking so you can stop disruptive key combinations globally or only inside selected Windows applications.
- Hotkey-driven typing automation for repeatable text actions, using either fixed text or clipboard content.
- A local tray-hosted dashboard for status, rules, typing actions, recent activity, and runtime settings.
- A local-first operating model that keeps configuration and control on the same machine instead of depending on a hosted service.

## Highlights In 0.1.0

- Shortcut rules can target specific processes, which makes it practical to block destructive or distracting shortcuts only where they are a problem.
- Typing automation uses Unicode input injection so text automation works with more than simple ASCII snippets.
- The service hosts a bundled dashboard locally, so the UI and the Windows runtime stay together in a single desktop-oriented workflow.
- Recent keyboard activity and runtime status are exposed through the dashboard, giving you a direct way to validate what was blocked and what was allowed through.

## What Ships In This Release

- `KeyMagic.exe`: a portable, self-contained single-file Windows application.
- `KeyMagic.exe.sha256`: a checksum file for download verification.
- Embedded dashboard assets and Key Magic branding bundled inside the executable, with no separate `wwwroot` payload required for release use.
- Local configuration stored at `%APPDATA%\KeyMagic\config.json`.

## Release Trust And Delivery

- Stable releases are rebuilt from a validated `main` commit instead of being published directly from an unreviewed branch state.
- The release flow publishes a SHA-256 checksum alongside the executable.
- The repository release automation supports Authenticode signing and GitHub provenance attestations when the repository secrets and platform support are in place.

## Packaging Notes

- This release is delivered as a portable executable, not an installer or MSIX package.
- `KeyMagic.Tester` remains in the repository for diagnostics and development validation, but it is not part of the shipped stable asset set.
- The intended public download experience for this release is a single executable plus checksum.

## Operational Notes

- Key Magic is Windows-first and designed around native keyboard handling behavior.
- On a new configuration, protection starts paused until you resume it from the tray menu or dashboard.
- The bundled dashboard is local-only and listens on `http://localhost:5199` by default.
- Some Windows-reserved shortcuts cannot be intercepted from user mode.
- The dashboard and API are local-only and intended for on-machine control of the running app.

## First Release Context

This is the curated baseline note for Key Magic. Later stable releases can rely on the normal auto-generated release-note flow, but for `v0.1.0` this override replaces the broader first-release summary that would otherwise include the full setup history leading to the initial public launch.
